term_inventory_hebrew.meaning: (Pilel) to be at ease, be at peace, rest, rest securely, be quiet

term_inventory_greek.meaning: worry, concern, anxiety; care, concern (Mt 13:22; Mk 4:19; Lk 8:14; 21:34); anxiety, anxious interest (2Cor 11:28; 1Pet 5:7). LSJ: care, thought, solicitude; plural: cares; concrete object of care or thought; pursuit, ambition (plural); anxious mind.
term_inventory_greek.lsj_entry: merimna, he: care, thought, solicitude; plural: cares. Rare in early Prose. Concrete: object of care or thought. Plural: pursuit, ambition. Anxious mind. (Cf. Sanskrit smarati remember, Latin memor.)

term_inventory_greek.meaning: anticipation, expectation; apprehension. A looking for, expectation, anticipation, apprehension (Lk 21:26); metonymy: expectation, what is expected or anticipated (Acts 12:11). LSJ: looking for, expectation, whether in hope or fear, but more commonly fear.
term_inventory_greek.lsj_entry: prosdokia, he: looking for, expectation, whether in hope or fear, but more commonly fear. Also: fulfilment of expectations raised.

term_inventory_greek.meaning: free from concern, free from care; free from care, or solicitude (Mt 28:14; 1Cor 7:32). LSJ: free from care, unconcerned. Passive: uncared for.
term_inventory_greek.lsj_entry: amerimnos, on: free from care, unconcerned. Passive: uncared for. Comparative: amerimnotes.

term_inventory_greek.meaning: free from anxiety; in the comparative, less anxiety; free from grief, or sorrow (Phil 2:28). LSJ: without pain. Active: causing no pain or grief, harmless.
term_inventory_greek.lsj_entry: alupos, on: without pain. Adverb alupos: live free from pain and sorrow. Active: causing no pain or grief, harmless.

verse_records.verse_text: When the cares of my heart are many, your consolations cheer my soul.
Search me, O God, and know my heart! Try me and know my thoughts!
As for what was sown among thorns, this is the one who hears the word, but the cares of the world and the deceitfulness of riches choke the word, and it proves unfruitful.
but the cares of the world and the deceitfulness of riches and the desires for other things enter in and choke the word, and it proves unfruitful.
And as for what fell among the thorns, they are those who hear, but as they go on their way they are choked by the cares and riches and pleasures of life, and their fruit does not mature.
But watch yourselves lest your hearts be weighed down with dissipation and drunkenness and cares of this life, and that day come upon you suddenly like a trap.
And, apart from other things, there is the daily pressure on me of my anxiety for all the churches.
casting all your anxieties on him, because he cares for you.
people fainting with fear and with foreboding of what is coming on the world. For the powers of the heavens will be shaken.
When Peter came to himself, he said, "Now I am sure that the Lord has sent his angel and rescued me from the hand of Herod and from all that the Jewish people were expecting."
There the prisoners are at ease together; they hear not the voice of the taskmaster.
but whoever listens to me will dwell secure and will be at ease, without dread of disaster.

Then fear not, O Jacob my servant, declares the Lord, nor be dismayed, O Israel; for behold, I will save you from far away, and your offspring from the land of their captivity. Jacob shall return and have quiet and ease, and none shall make him afraid.

But fear not, O Jacob my servant, nor be dismayed, O Israel, for behold, I will save you from far away, and your offspring from the land of their captivity. Jacob shall return and have quiet and ease, and none shall make him afraid.

Moab has been at ease from his youth and has settled on his dregs; he has not been emptied from vessel to vessel, nor has he gone into exile; so his taste remains in him, and his scent is not changed.
And if this comes to the governor's ears, we will satisfy him and keep you out of trouble.
I want you to be free from anxieties. The unmarried man is anxious about the things of the Lord, how to please the Lord.
I am the more eager to send him, therefore, that you may rejoice at seeing him again, and that I may be less anxious.

cross_registry_links.note: de.a.gah (H1674) appears in both the anxiety (007) and sorrow (154) word sets.
da.ag (H1672) appears in both the anxiety (007) and fear (061) word sets.

alupos occurs about 1x. Only one verse record available (Phil 2:28). Assessments will be limited.
